title: Default DatabaseSeeder can create known super-admin credentials outside dev data setup
reasoning: The default db:seed path calls FamilyMemberSeeder, which falls back to public email/password values and creates a super-admin plus demo users. If the default seeder is run against any non-isolated environment without explicit admin config, the application gets a known privileged login.
reproduction: Run php artisan db:seed in an environment without ADMIN_EMAIL and ADMIN_PASSWORD configured. DatabaseSeeder calls FamilyMemberSeeder, which creates admin@family.test with password password and is_super_admin true.
recommendation: Gate demo seeding to local/testing environments or require explicit credentials before creating privileged users. Keep production bootstrapping on ProductionSeeder and make DatabaseSeeder refuse to create known-password accounts outside safe environments.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: ProductionSeederTest verifies ProductionSeeder avoids demo contribution/payment data, but no test runs DatabaseSeeder under a production-like environment or asserts that the known fallback admin is blocked.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that sets the environment to production, runs DatabaseSeeder, and asserts admin@family.test is not created and the seeder fails or skips demo users explicitly.
minimumFixScope: Add an environment/config guard around DatabaseSeeder or FamilyMemberSeeder and cover it with one seeder safety test.
